articles.title: Stocks Fall, Oil Rises on Doubts Over US-Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Wall Street’s rebound attempt failed, with stocks falling as oil rose after doubts resurfaced about an imminent US-Iran deal that would revive energy flows through the key Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Oil Fluctuates on Doubts Over US, Iran Stance to End Conflict
articles.summary: Oil swung between gains and losses with new questions emerging from both the US and Iran on the progress of peace negotiations aimed at reopening the critical Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Adani Enterprises Reaches $275 Million Settlement With Treasury
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises Ltd. agreed to pay $275 million over a probe by the Office of Foreign Assets Control, putting the Indian conglomerate a step closer to resolving its legal troubles in the US.

articles.title: Zambia Seen Reaping Record Corn Harvest, Bringing Gains and Risk
articles.summary: Zambia’s staple corn harvest will exceed 4.1 million tons this year, according to the country’s millers association, a second straight record that could help cool inflation but also poses fiscal risks

articles.title: Over 34,000 PG&E Customers Without Power on California Fire Risk
articles.summary: More than 34,000 homes and businesses remain without power across PG&E Corp.’s service area after a weekend outage due in part to dry and windy conditions that raised the risk of wild fires.

articles.title: Gold Gains as Yields Push Lower on Renewed US-Iran Deal Hopes
articles.summary: Gold advanced as bond yields pushed lower on renewed hopes for a breakthrough in the standoff between the US and Iran over the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: LIRR Strike Jams Roads, Prompts Long Commutes as Talks Continue
articles.summary:
Long Island workers making their way into New York City grappled with long commutes Monday as the region’s rail road entered its third strike day.

 (Source: Bloomberg)
